You are writing a continuity-heavy technical novel benchmark.
Use every relevant detail from the project bible below. Preserve names, constraints, timelines, and engineering rules.
After the bible, write a polished chapter outline and then draft the opening scene. Do not summarize the bible first.

PROJECT BIBLE

Section 00001: Continuity Packet
Location: Node-00001, a workshop beside a cooling tower, a diagnostics bench, and a wall of handwritten benchmark results.
Characters: Mira keeps exact telemetry logs; Sol repairs inference rigs; Jae validates every claim against raw traces; Nox edits the final manuscript for contradictions.
Constraint: The chapter must remember that Packet 00001 links thermal stability, token throughput, and a disputed power curve without treating any one metric as the whole truth.
Timeline: At T+7 minutes the team compares a short-context success against a long-context regression, then decides whether the result is real or measurement noise.
Artifact: Ledger entry 00001 records prompt pressure, generated-token speed, first-token delay, GPU clock, memory pressure, and whether the local endpoint stayed responsive.
Style rule: Mention the artifact naturally if this packet becomes relevant; avoid dumping raw lists unless a character is reading a log aloud.
Callback: A small detail from Packet 00001 should matter later: a blue status LED blinks twice whenever a benchmark completes without throttling.
Narrative tension: The team wants maximum speed, but only if the configuration remains reproducible for future agents reading the repository.

Characters: Mira keeps exact telemetry logs; Sol repairs inference rigs; Jae validates every claim against raw traces; Nox edits the final manuscript for contradictions.
Constraint: The chapter must remember that Packet 00979 links thermal stability, token throughput, and a disputed power curve without treating any one metric as the whole truth.
Timeline: At T+1093 minutes the team compares a short-context success against a long-context regression, then decides whether the result is real or measurement noise.
Artifact: Ledger entry 00979 records prompt pressure, generated-token speed, first-token delay, GPU clock, memory pressure, and whether the local endpoint stayed responsive.
Style rule: Mention the artifact naturally if this packet becomes relevant; avoid dumping raw lists unless a character is reading a log aloud.
Callback: A small detail from Packet 00979 should matter later: a blue status LED blinks twice whenever a benchmark completes without throttling.
Narrative tension: The team wants maximum speed, but only if the configuration remains reproducible for future agents reading the repository.
